title: Aphyllon tuberosum
taxonomy: Aphyllon tuberosum is classified as a genus within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and morphology. Aphyllon tuberosum is a type of root parasite, meaning it obtains its nutrients by connecting with the roots of other plants. It belongs to the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the gentian and loganiaceae families. The classification of Aphyllon tuberosum is subject to ongoing research and potential reclassification as new information becomes available.

parts: Aphyllon tuberosum, also known as Larkspur, the medicinal parts of this plant are primarily its roots and tubers. preparations: Aphyllon tuberosum is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine, particularly in the United States and Mexico. side_effects: Aphyllon tuberosum, also known as false cambrica, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Native American and European folk remedies. However, like any plant-based remedy, Aphyllon tuberosum may cause side effects in some individuals. These side effects can include allergic reactions, such as skin irritation or anaphylaxis, and gastrointestinal issues like nausea or vomiting. Furthermore, using Aphyllon tuberosum without proper medical supervision can lead to interactions with other medications or exacerbate underlying health conditions. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Aphyllon tuberosum or any other plant-based remedy.
